segments.text: MICROTUBULES

MICROTUBULES
Subunit = heterodimer of a- and b-tubulin
Tube of 13 protofilaments
Polar organization: (+) and (-) ends

MICROTUBULE ORGANIZING CENTER
Often 1 per cell
Site of microtubule nucleation
(-) ends at MTOC
Types – centrosomes, spindle poles, basal bodies

CENTROSOME
Pair of centrioles
each with 9 MT triplets
Pericentriolar material
contains many g-TuRCs (and other material)

g-TuRC
g-tubulin ring complex
Forms ring that nucleates new microtubules

MICROTUBULE DYNAMICS
b-tubulin binds and hydrolyzes GTP
GTP cap stabilizes (+) end

MICROTUBULE TOXINS
Colchicine
prevents polymerization
meadow saffron
Taxol
stabilizes microtubules
pacific yew bark
common anti-cancer drug

MICROTUBULE MOLECULAR MOTORS
2 families
dyneins (all (-) end directed)
kinesins (most (+) end directed)

MOTOR “WALKING”
Energy from ATP hydrolysis
Kinesin – 8 nm steps (adjacent b-tubulins)
Dynein – 8 nm (or multiples of 8 nm) steps

DYNEIN SPEED
Dynein
~ 20 x 40 nm
~ 2 mm/sec
~ 50 body lengths/sec
Bugatti Veyron Super Sport
4.5 m long
268 mph top speed
26.4 body lengths/sec

MOTOR CARGOES
Vesicles and organelles throughout cell

CILIA/FLAGELLA
Cilia and eukaryotic flagella = same structure
Hair-like extension of cell surface

CILIARY STRUCTURE
5-20 mm long
200-300 nm wide
700-1000 different proteins
Axoneme = cilium without membrane

CILIARY MICROTUBULES
Grow from basal body
Basal body: 9 triplet MTs
A and B MTs extend into cilium
(+) ends at tip
(-) ends at cell body

AXONEMAL COMPONENTS
9+2 organization of MTs:
9 outer doublets + central pair of singlets
Dynein arms – outer (OAD) and inner (IAD)
Nexin links
Radial spokes
Central sheath

AXONEMAL DYNEINS
Walk toward (-) end of doublets
Outer arm dynein
first microtubule motor isolated
Ian Gibbons (1963) -Tetrahymena cilia

CILIARY BENDING
Adjacent doublets – one track and one cargo for dyneins
Linking proteins convert sliding into bending
What if all dyneins active at once?

REGULATION OF CILIARY BENDING
Only dyneins on one half of ciliary cross-section active at a given time

CILIOGENESIS
Growth of cilium
1000s of components synthesized in cell body
components added to distal tip

Intraflagellar Transport
Mechanism of cilia growth
Joel Rosenbaum Lab
Anterograde motor – kinesin 2
Retrograde motor – dynein 2
IFT trains carry cargoes
Is IFT TEXTBOOK MODEL CORRECT?Yes, but too simple.
